feature: Recommendation Completion Tracking with GEO Score Impact Measurement
workflow_rationale: This is a new feature development adding completion tracking, before/after GEO score measurement, effectiveness calculation, and user feedback to an existing recommendation system. It involves database schema changes, new API endpoints, frontend UI components, and analytics logic.

phases.name: Database Schema Updates
phases.description: Add completion tracking, score measurement, and feedback fields to recommendations table

subtasks.description: Update recommendations schema with completion tracking fields
implementation_notes: Add fields: startedAt (timestamp), baselineScore (integer), postImplementationScore (integer), scoreImprovement (integer), effectivenessScore (integer 0-100), userRating (integer 1-5), userFeedback (text), feedbackAt (timestamp)
Note: 'status' and 'completedAt' already exist in schema
Use Drizzle conventions: timestamp('field_name', { withTimezone: true }), integer('field_name')
All new fields should be nullable since existing recommendations won't have this data
subtasks.notes: Added 8 new fields to recommendations schema: startedAt, baselineScore, postImplementationScore, scoreImprovement, effectivenessScore, userRating, userFeedback, feedbackAt. Migration 0005_cloudy_random.sql generated successfully. All fields nullable to preserve existing data.

implementation_notes: Use npm run db:migrate to apply the migration
Verify in Drizzle Studio (npm run db:studio) that new columns exist
Test that existing recommendation records are not affected
subtasks.notes: Migration successfully applied using custom migration script (scripts/apply-completion-tracking-migration.ts). All 8 completion tracking columns verified in database: started_at, baseline_score, post_implementation_score, score_improvement, effectiveness_score, user_rating, user_feedback, feedback_at. Note: npm run db:migrate has a pre-existing issue with migration tracking, so custom script was used with IF NOT EXISTS clauses.

phases.name: Effectiveness Calculation Logic
phases.description: Build utility functions for calculating effectiveness scores and improvement metrics

implementation_notes: Implement calculateEffectivenessScore(baselineScore, postScore, implementationDays, priority) -> 0-100
Formula: 70% score improvement, 20% time efficiency, 10% priority bonus
Implement calculateScoreImprovement(postScore, baselineScore) -> delta (can be negative)
Handle edge cases: negative improvements, missing scores, division by zero
Export TypeScript types for calculation inputs/outputs
subtasks.notes: Created effectiveness calculation utilities in src/lib/analytics/effectiveness.ts with comprehensive test coverage (41 tests, all passing). Implemented: calculateEffectivenessScore, calculateScoreImprovement, calculateTimeEfficiencyScore, calculateEffectivenessResult, calculateAggregateMetrics, getEffectivenessLevel, getEffectivenessDescription. Formula: 70% score improvement, 20% time efficiency, 10% priority bonus.

subtasks.description: Write unit tests for effectiveness calculations
implementation_notes: Test normal case: baseline=65, post=78, days=7, priority=high -> effectiveness ~85
Test negative improvement: baseline=70, post=60 -> effectiveness=0
Test zero improvement: baseline=80, post=80 -> effectiveness based on time/priority only
Test boundary: baseline=0, post=100 -> max effectiveness
Test invalid inputs: negative scores, null values
subtasks.notes: Unit tests for effectiveness calculations already exist and pass (41 tests). Tests were created as part of subtask-2-1 implementation. All required test cases covered: calculateScoreImprovement (positive/negative/zero/max/invalid), calculateTimeEfficiencyScore (early/on-time/late by priority), calculateEffectivenessScore (high effectiveness, negative/zero improvement, max improvement, boundary cases, invalid inputs, priority levels), calculateEffectivenessResult (complete result object), calculateAggregateMetrics (averages, null values, empty array), getEffectivenessLevel/getEffectivenessDescription, clamp helper, and constants verification.

phases.name: API Endpoints
phases.description: Create/update API routes for completion tracking, score capture, and feedback submission

subtasks.description: Update status endpoint to handle score capture
implementation_notes: Extend existing validation schema to accept optional baselineScore, postImplementationScore
When status changes to 'in_progress': require baselineScore, set startedAt timestamp
When status changes to 'completed': require postImplementationScore, calculate scoreImprovement and effectivenessScore using lib/analytics/effectiveness
Return updated recommendation with all new fields in response
Maintain existing authentication/authorization checks (getOrganizationId, brand ownership)
subtasks.notes: Updated status endpoint to handle score capture. Extended validation schema with baselineScore and postImplementationScore fields. Added validation to require baselineScore when transitioning to in_progress and postImplementationScore when completing. Calculates scoreImprovement and effectivenessScore automatically on completion using the analytics/effectiveness utilities. Maps priority enum (critical/high/medium/low) to effectiveness calculation priority type. Clears score tracking fields when reverting status. TypeScript compilation and linting pass. Committed as 979053b5.

subtasks.description: Update feedback API to persist to database
implementation_notes: Replace in-memory feedbackManager with database persistence
Update submitFeedback action to write userRating, userFeedback, feedbackAt to recommendations table
Use Drizzle ORM update query with eq(recommendations.id, recommendationId)
Validate rating is 1-5, feedback is optional text
Return updated recommendation in response
Keep authentication checks from original implementation
subtasks.notes: Updated feedback API to persist to database using recommendations table fields (userRating, userFeedback, feedbackAt). Follows pattern from status endpoint with org authorization via brands table. Simplified API to core functionality: GET retrieves feedback by brandId/recommendationId, POST submits feedback with rating 1-5 and optional text.

implementation_notes: GET endpoint accepting optional brandId query parameter
Query recommendations table for completed recommendations (status='completed')
Calculate aggregate metrics: totalCompleted, averageEffectiveness, averageScoreImprovement, topPerformers (top 5 by effectivenessScore)
Filter by brandId and organization ownership
Return JSON with metrics and list of top performing recommendations
Use Drizzle ORM select with where, orderBy, limit

phases.name: Frontend UI Components
phases.description: Build React components for completion tracking, score input, and feedback collection

checks: Start Implementation button visible for pending recommendations
Score input field appears when starting
Mark Completed button appears for in-progress recommendations

implementation_notes: Accept recommendation prop with id, status, baselineScore, postImplementationScore
Show different UI based on status: pending -> 'Start Implementation' button, in_progress -> score input + 'Mark Completed', completed -> scores display
Use useUpdateRecommendationStatus hook from useRecommendations.ts
For 'Start Implementation': show dialog with number input for baseline GEO score (0-100 validation)
For 'Mark Completed': show dialog with number input for post-implementation score
Call API with optimistic updates
Use Radix UI Dialog component, Button from src/components/ui
Apply Tailwind CSS with status badge colors: pending=gray, in_progress=blue, completed=green

checks: Feedback button visible for completed recommendations

implementation_notes: Accept recommendationId prop
Use Radix UI Dialog for modal
Implement star rating: 5 clickable star icons (use lucide-react Star icon)
Textarea for optional text feedback
Submit button disabled until rating selected
Call /api/recommendations/feedback POST with action='submit'
Show success toast/message on submission
Skip button closes dialog without saving

checks: Metrics cards display: total completed, average effectiveness, average improvement
Top performers list shows top 5 recommendations
No NaN or undefined values displayed

implementation_notes: Accept optional brandId prop
Fetch data from /api/recommendations/effectiveness using React Query
Display aggregate metrics in card layout: Total Completed, Avg Effectiveness (0-100), Avg Score Improvement (+X points)
Show top 5 performing recommendations with title, effectiveness score, score improvement
Use Tailwind CSS card components
Handle loading state with skeleton loaders
Handle empty state (no completed recommendations yet)
Format numbers: round to 1 decimal, add + prefix for positive improvements

subtasks.description: Update recommendations page to integrate new components

checks: CompletionTracker visible on each recommendation card
Feedback button visible for completed recommendations

implementation_notes: Import CompletionTracker and FeedbackDialog components
Add CompletionTracker to each recommendation card in the list
Add FeedbackDialog with conditional rendering (show for completed only)
Ensure existing recommendation display functionality is preserved
Test with different recommendation statuses

phases.name: Integration & End-to-End Testing
phases.description: Wire all components together and verify complete user flows

steps: Verify effectiveness score calculated and shown

implementation_notes: Use Playwright to automate the full user workflow
Create test data: seed database with pending recommendation
Test validation: reject invalid scores (negative, >100, non-numeric)
Test calculations: verify scoreImprovement = postScore - baselineScore
Test edge case: negative improvement (post < baseline)
Clean up test data after test run
Create test data: seed completed recommendation
Test star rating interaction (click stars 1-5)
Test optional feedback text
Test 'Skip' button (closes without saving)
Verify database persistence: query recommendations table after submission
Test that feedback can only be submitted once per recommendation

subtasks.description: Verify effectiveness report displays correct metrics

steps: Seed database with 10 completed recommendations with varying effectiveness scores
Verify top 5 performers list shows highest effectiveness scores first

implementation_notes: Create comprehensive test dataset with known metrics
Calculate expected averages manually for comparison
Test edge cases: 0 completed recommendations (empty state), 1 recommendation, 100+ recommendations
Test filtering by brandId if implemented
Verify no NaN or undefined values in UI
Test sorting of top performers

parallel_groups.reason: Effectiveness calculations can be built while database migration is being tested (no dependency)

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Sequential execution recommended due to tight dependencies between phases

acceptance_criteria: All existing tests pass after schema changes
New effectiveness calculation logic has 100% test coverage
API endpoints validate inputs and handle errors gracefully
UI components work with different recommendation statuses
Database migration runs without data loss

verification_steps.name: Unit Tests - Effectiveness Calculations
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All calculation tests pass (normal, negative, edge cases)

verification_steps.name: Integration Tests - API Endpoints
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All API tests pass (status update, feedback, effectiveness)

verification_steps.name: E2E Tests - Complete Workflows
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Full completion and feedback workflows pass

verification_steps.name: Database Migration Verification
verification_steps.expected_outcome: New columns exist, existing data preserved, constraints valid

verification_steps.name: Browser Verification - Completion UI
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Can start implementation, enter scores, mark completed, view effectiveness

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk due to database schema changes affecting existing data model. Requires comprehensive testing at all levels (unit, integration, E2E) to ensure data integrity and correct calculations. No security-sensitive operations or external integrations.

checks: Feedback button visible for completed recommendations
Skip button closes dialog without saving
Schema migration creates all new columns (startedAt, baselineScore, postImplementationScore, scoreImprovement, effectivenessScore, userRating, userFeedback, feedbackAt)
Status enum still contains: pending, in_progress, completed, dismissed
Timestamps populate correctly on status changes
Score calculations stored accurately (scoreImprovement = post - baseline)
